faqs.question: What is Zomato?
faqs.answer: Zomato is a food delivery platform that allows you to order food from restaurants and get it delivered to your door.

content.question: What data is collected?
content.answer: We only collect the minimum needed to run the app. When you open the app, we record the equivalent of: "Someone using Jomato version X opened the app today." That's an anonymous count (we don't know who you are). If you log in with Zomato, we store your Zomato session on your device only so you stay logged in; we don't collect your name, email, or phone beyond what Zomato needs for login.

content.question: Why do you collect that data?
content.answer: So we know how many people use Jomato and which app versions are in use. That helps us decide when to fix bugs and when to suggest updates. It's like knowing "how many people opened the app today" without knowing who they are.

content.question: Is Jomato affiliated with Zomato?
content.answer: No. Jomato is not affiliated with Zomato. Use at your own risk.

content.question: Do you use my location?
content.answer: We don't use your phone's GPS. Food Rescue uses the area or city you've set in your Zomato account (your saved addresses). We don't track or store your precise location.

content.question: Where does my data go?
content.answer: The anonymous "app opened" info is used only to understand usage (e.g. how many people use which version). When you use features that need Zomato (like Food Rescue), those requests go to Zomato. Your Zomato login stays on your device; we don't store your password anywhere.

content.question: Why do you need notification, battery, and install permissions?
content.answer: Notifications: So we can notify you for Food Rescue. Battery: So the rescue feature can run in the background. Install: So you can install updates from the in-app update prompt if you choose to.
